calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexandra Ayoub and Michael C. Ferguson, for and with Petitioners David Sims and Kenneth Sims.
The Court asked if Ms. Ayoub had anything to add in support of the petition. Ms. Ayoub replied that in hindsight the Petitioners should not have made the advanced distributions of the assigned interests to Inheritance Funding Co. and Approval Inheritance Cash, Inc. without prior court approval, but that no one was harmed by the early distributions. The Petition re Approval of Accounting #1 was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Continued To Aug-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-06-2015 Continued To Jul-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexandra Ayoub for and with David Sims and Kenneth Sims (Administrators).

Ms. Ayoub stated that they sold the house, that the Probate Referee now has the inventory and appraisal and that she hopes to have it back within the next few weeks. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal was continued to January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexandra Ayoub for (petitioner) Kenneth Sims & David Sims.

Ms. Ayoub stated that she filed a declaration this morning explaining why Steven Sims has not been heard from, spoke of Steven's medical condition and stated that no one has seen him for quite a while, that Steven was last known to live in Sacramento, however, the mail comes back from that address which is why they used the brother's house as a mailing address. The court expressed concern as to why there is no request to dispense with notice, advised counsel that an attorney by the name of Stanley Switzer represented a Steven Sims in a personal injury case in and raised the issue of bond or serving with limited authority. Ms. Ayoub stated that the family preferred to have a bond for the full value of the estate. The petition for letters of administration was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Continued To Aug-05-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Continued To Jul-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
